content.claim: Teacher shortages in Hungary are greatest in rural areas, with half of all teachers aged 50+ in 2024/2025 and only 7.2% under 30, and the proportion of unqualified teachers in disadvantaged (primarily rural) schools 2-3 times higher than in non-disadvantaged schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Age distribution and qualification gaps are documented at national/regional level but not disaggregated specifically for 6-/8-year gimnázium specialist staff
Rural teacher shortage is partly attributable to demographic aging and location preference, not solely to network structure
No direct measurement of whether teacher capacity constraints limit expansion of early-selection tracks in rural areas
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
